The language has been changed. Please re: The language has been changed. Please restart the application for the changes to take full effect.

bed_tab.instructions:
IMPORTANT:
• FRONT side of the bed - closer to you
• BACK side - further from you

1. Load the printer.cfg file
2. TURN THE SCREWS HOLD THE NUTS!!! Use visualization to analyze the bed
3. Follow the adjustment recommendations
bed_tab.initial_recommendations:
Recommendations for bed leveling will be displayed here
after loading the configuration file.

To begin, press the
'Load Configuration' button.
bed_tab.action_belts: 1. Synchronise Z lead screws (bring delta below {:.3f}mm)
bed_tab.belts_disabled: Z lead screw stage disabled in settings.
bed_tab.tape_no_adjustments: Area is within tolerance – no tape needed.
bed_tab.workflow_disabled_hint: Some stages are disabled in settings.

shaper_tab.analysis_complete: Analysis complete. Recommended shaper parameters updated.
shaper_tab.copied:
Configuration copied to clipboard.
Paste it into your printer.cfg file.
shaper_tab.help_text:
Input Shaper is a technology that helps reduce vibrations in a 3D printer during printing.

Vibrations can cause defects on the model, such as 'ghosting'. Input Shaper analyzes the printer's movements and adjusts them to minimize these vibrations.

How it works?
1. Load accelerometer data.
2. The program selects the best 'shaper'.
3. Copy the settings.

Result: smoother printing and fewer defects!
shaper_tab.select_and_copy: Select a shaper and copy the settings!
shaper_tab.export_no_data: No plots available yet. Load accelerometer data first.

settings_tab.no_shapers_found: No shaper files were found on the printer.
settings_tab.ssh_connection_desc: SSH connection allows automatic loading of configuration files and calibration data directly from your printer
settings_tab.connection_requirements: For connection, the following is required:
settings_tab.interpolation_factor_desc: Interpolation factor determines the smoothness of 3D visualization (higher value = prettier graph, but more load)
settings_tab.hardware_info:
• Screw pitch determines how much height changes per full turn
• Tape thickness affects the calculation of layers for compensation
• Min/max adjustment limits the range of suggested changes
settings_tab.thresholds_info:
1. If deviation > screw threshold, the program suggests screw adjustment
2. If after screw adjustment deviation > tape threshold,
   the program suggests additional tape correction
3. If deviation < screw threshold but > tape threshold,
   the program suggests only tape correction
settings_tab.visualization_info:
• Interpolation factor determines the smoothness of 3D visualization
  (higher value = prettier graph, but more load)

• Display settings allow choosing units for screw adjustment instructions (minutes and/or degrees)
settings_tab.display_options_error:
At least one display option (minutes or degrees) must be enabled.
Both options have been enabled by default.
settings_tab.environment_info:
Temperature difference between measurement and printing can warp the bed.
Use the coefficient to control the strength and direction of the simulated dome (negative values invert the curvature).

visual_rec.title: Visual Recommendations
visual_rec.temperature_map_hint: Red areas rise after heating, blue areas drop. Compare with a hot mesh to confirm.
visual_rec.temperature_profile_line: PEI {pei:.2f} mm • Steel {steel:.2f} mm • γ={gamma:.2f} • β={beta:.2f}
visual_rec.instructions:
IMPORTANT! Bed orientation:
• FRONT side - closer to you (where the control panel is)
• BACK side - further from you (rear of the printer)

1. Locate the four corner screws on your bed (see screw adjustment scheme).
2. Check the "Mode" caption above the scheme — it tells you whether you are turning screws or nuts.
3. Follow the rotation arrows on the cards; they already account for the chosen mode.
4. After adjusting screws, follow the tape application scheme for final correction.
5. After all adjustments, re-measure the bed to verify results.
visual_rec.hardware_settings_summary: Hardware settings: screw pitch {pitch:.2f} mm • min adjustment {min_adjust:.2f} mm • max adjustment {max_adjust:.2f} mm • tape thickness {tape:.2f} mm
visual_rec.drag_hint: Tip: drag the tab headers to rearrange the recommendation cards.
visual_rec.temperature_summary: Temperature: measured at {measurement:.1f}°C → target {target:.1f}°C • thermal coefficient {coeff:.5f} mm/°C
visual_rec.belt_stage_description: Use the guidance below to balance the three Z lead screws before fine screw and tape adjustments.
visual_rec.belt_instruction_overview:
Sequence:
1. Power off the printer
2. Lay printer on its back
3. Loosen the tensioner lock bolts before turning the screws
visual_rec.belt_instruction_finish:
After adjustment:
• Re-tension the belts
• Return the printer upright
• Re-run the bed mesh
visual_rec.instructions_short: Work through the stages in order. Each panel tells you exactly what to adjust.
visual_rec.instructions_heatmap_hint: Dark red means the bed is high, dark blue means low. Focus on the brightest zones first.
visual_rec.belt_extra_tip: Tip: release the synchronising belt clamp before turning the shaft, then tighten it again.
visual_rec.belt_rotation_ccw: Turn the shaft counterclockwise (raises the gantry)
visual_rec.belt_rotation_cw: Turn the shaft clockwise (lowers the gantry)
visual_rec.tape_instruction_2: • Apply tape exactly at the highlighted cells
visual_rec.tape_instruction_3: • The number shows how many layers to stack
visual_rec.tape_instruction_4: • Re-measure the mesh after applying tape
visual_rec.screw_legend:
• Clockwise - LOWERS the corner
• Counterclockwise - RAISES the corner
• 1 turn = 60 min = 360°
visual_rec.stage_screw_details: Adjust screws to minimise residual tilt.
visual_rec.stage_tape_details: Compensate remaining low spots with tape layers.
visual_rec.stage_temperature_details: Estimated deformation caused by temperature change.
visual_rec.screw_animation_hint: Watch the animation: it shows which way to turn each screw.
visual_rec.temperature_instructions: Heat the bed from {measurement:.1f}°C to {target:.1f}°C. PEI expands about {coeff:.1e} per °C, so wait a few minutes for the plate to stabilise.
visual_rec.temperature_expected: Expected dome change ≈ {delta:.3f} mm across the bed.
visual_rec.temperature_tip: After heating, run another mesh at printing temperature to confirm the result.

help.initial: This is the baseline mesh before any corrections.
help.belts: Synchronise the front Z shafts first. Follow the highlighted arrows to raise or lower the corners.
help.screws: Use the adjustment screws for fine tuning. Clockwise lowers the corner, counterclockwise raises it.
help.tape: Apply aluminium tape to the highlighted grid cells. The number indicates how many layers to stack.
help.temperature: Shows how the bed may warp when heating from measurement temperature to target temperature.

visual_rec.belt_threshold_line: Belt adjustments trigger above {threshold:.2f} mm (one tooth ≈ {tooth:.2f} mm).
visual_rec.screw_threshold_line: Screw adjustments trigger above {threshold:.2f} mm (pitch {pitch:.2f} mm).

about.title: About
about.description:
Application for analysis and calibration
of 3D printer bed

• Bed level analysis
• Calibration recommendations
• Input shaper analysis

Drop format not supported: Dropped file format is not supported.
Drag and drop is not available on this p: Drag and drop is not available on this platform. Use 'Load Configuration'.

footer.author: by
egg: Your belts are perfectly tensioned... probably.

dialogs.drop_unsupported: Only printer.cfg (.cfg) and accelerometer CSV files are supported.

theme.not_implemented: Theme switching will appear in a later update.

author.title: About the author

dashboard.header: Welcome back! Here's a quick summary
dashboard.workflow_pending: Run bed analysis to populate workflow.
dashboard.shaper_pending: Load accelerometer logs to compute shapers.

activity.title: Recent activity
activity.placeholder: After loading a configuration you'll see workflow progress and exports here.

quick.title: Quick actions
quick.description: Jump directly to the most used tools.

ssh.cli_desc: Run this command in the terminal to download data from the printer and open the web app:
ssh.manual_desc: Alternatively, upload files from your computer or drag them onto this page.

data.title: Data Import
data.description: Load printer.cfg and shaper CSV files into the app. Choose the method that suits your setup.
data.flash_hint: If your printer has stock firmware without SSH access, use the service menu to export printer.cfg to a flash drive.
data.flash_step_1: Insert a flash drive into the printer.
data.flash_step_2: Press the (i) icon to open the Machine Info screen.
data.flash_step_3: Press and hold the "Machine Info" text for about 10 seconds until the service menu appears.
data.flash_step_4: Go to the Test tab, then inside "Change printer.base.cfg" press [get].
data.flash_step_5: Remove the flash drive and insert it into your computer.
data.flash_step_6: Click the button below to load the printer.cfg file.
data.ssh_hint: Use the CLI utility to automatically download printer.cfg and shaper data via SSH.
data.ssh_paths:
File paths on the printer (connect as root via SSH/WinSCP):
• printer.cfg: /opt/config/printer.cfg or /root/printer_data/config/printer.cfg or /usr/data/config/printer.cfg
• Shaper CSVs: /tmp/calibration_data_*.csv

recommendations.title: Calibration workflow
recommendations.placeholder: Workflow recommendations will appear here after loading a mesh.
recommendations.modal_placeholder: Detailed visual guidance is available in the dedicated dialog.

shaper.placeholder: Load accelerometer CSV logs to visualise resonances and fetch recommended shaper settings.
shaper.csv_hint: Drag & drop or click to select accelerometer CSV files. Files with '_x' in name are loaded as X axis, others as Y axis.
shaper.no_data: No data loaded for this axis
shaper.result_placeholder: No analysis yet. Load a CSV file to compute recommendations.
shaper.error: Failed to analyse the CSV file.

printer.ad5x_hint: AD5X uses hold-screw mode — screw rotation is reversed in recommendations.

visual.figure_placeholder: Select a stage to view the mesh and highlights.

visual.hardware_summary: Hardware settings: screw pitch {pitch:.2f} mm • min adjustment {min_adjust:.2f} mm • max adjustment {max_adjust:.2f} mm • tape thickness {tape:.2f} mm

hints.instructions_short: Work through the stages in order. Each card explains what to adjust.

belts.threshold: Belt adjustments trigger above {threshold:.2f} mm (≈ {tooth:.2f} mm per tooth).
belts.overview:
Sequence:
1. Power off the printer
2. Lay the printer on its back
3. Loosen the tensioner lock bolts before turning the shafts
belts.tip: Tip: release the synchronising belt clamp before turning the shaft, then tighten it again.

screws.threshold: Screw adjustments trigger above {threshold:.2f} mm (pitch {pitch:.2f} mm).
screws.legend:
• Clockwise lowers the corner
• Counterclockwise raises the corner
• 1 turn = 60 minutes = 360°

tape.title: Apply aluminium tape to the highlighted squares:
tape.step_2: 2. Apply tape exactly at the highlighted cells
tape.step_3: 3. The number shows how many layers to stack
tape.step_4: 4. Re-measure the mesh after applying tape

temperature.summary: Temperature: measured at {measurement:.1f}°C → target {target:.1f}°C • coefficient {coeff:.5f} mm/°C
temperature.instructions: Heat the bed from {measurement:.1f}°C to {target:.1f}°C. PEI expands about {coeff:.1e} per °C; allow a few minutes for the plate to stabilise.
temperature.profile: Materials: PEI {pei:.2f} mm, steel {steel:.2f} mm, chamber factor γ={chamber:.2f}
temperature.tip: After heating, run another mesh at printing temperature to confirm the result.

belt.instr_1: Turn off the printer and carefully flip it on its back
belt.instr_2: Loosen the belt tensioner bolt, then the grub screw on the pulley
belt.instr_3: Turn each shaft pulley by the indicated teeth in the shown direction
belt.instr_4: Tighten everything back up and re-measure the mesh

screw.legend:
• Clockwise lowers the corner
• Counterclockwise raises the corner
• 1 turn = 60 minutes = 360°
screw.instr_intro: Remove the PEI build plate from the bed.
screw.instr_hold_nut: Hold the nut from below with a wrench. Turn the hex screw from above with an Allen key.
screw.instr_hold_screw: Turn the nut from below with a wrench, looking at the bed from above the printer.
screw.instr_direction: Clockwise (viewed from above) lowers the corner, counterclockwise raises it.
screw.instr_after: After adjusting, re-measure the mesh to check the result.

tape.legend: Number shows how many tape layers to stack.
tape.instr_2: Cut pieces to match the size of the highlighted cells
tape.instr_3: Stick the indicated number of layers at each position
tape.instr_4: Re-measure the mesh to verify the result

help.initial: Original bed mesh before any corrections.
help.belts: Synchronise Z lead screws to reduce front-to-back tilt.
help.screws: Fine-tune leveling screws at each corner.
help.tape: Apply aluminium tape to low spots.
help.temperature: Predict thermal warp at printing temperature.

temperature_profile_line: PEI {pei:.2f} mm • Steel {steel:.2f} mm • γ={gamma:.2f} • β={beta:.2f}
